name: Les Adverbes en -ment — Adverbs of Manner

cards.grammarNote: heureux (m.) → heureuse (f. form) + -ment = heureusement. Most -ment adverbs are formed from the feminine form of the adjective: heureux → heureuse → heureusement.
triste has the same masculine and feminine form → triste + -ment = tristement. When the masculine form ends in a vowel, add -ment directly to the masculine form.
nerveux (m.) → nerveuse (f.) + -ment = nerveusement. The -x → -se pattern: heureux/heureuse, nerveux/nerveuse, sérieux/sérieuse.
doux (m.) → douce (f.) + -ment = doucement. An irregular feminine: doux → douce (not douxe).
Some manner expressions use avec + noun instead of an adverb: avec colère (with anger), avec joie (with joy), avec patience. This is natural and sometimes preferred over long adverbs.
clair (m.) → claire (f.) + -ment = clairement. Adjectives ending in a vowel in the masculine form: add -ment directly to the masculine: vrai → vraiment, poli → poliment.
calme (same m./f.) + -ment = calmement. Note position: in passé composé, manner adverbs usually follow the past participle (unlike short adverbs like bien/mal/vite which come before it).
bruyant (m.) → bruyante (f.) → bruyamment. Adjectives ending in -ant/-ent form adverbs with -amment/-emment (both pronounced like 'amman'): bruyant → bruyamment, évident → évidemment.
Some adverbs ending in a vowel + -ment add a circumflex: passionné → passionnément (though this is being simplified in modern usage). s'exprimer = to express oneself (reflexive).

cards.back: Il a hoché la tête lentement
cards.grammarNote: lent (m.) → lente (f.) + -ment = lentement. hocher la tête = to nod. Adverbs usually come after the past participle in compound tenses.
poli (m.) ends in a vowel → add -ment directly (no need for feminine form): poliment. Other examples: absolu → absolument, vrai → vraiment.
vrai (m.) ends in a vowel → vraiment. Note: short adverbs like vraiment, bien, mal, vite typically come before the past participle in compound tenses, unlike long -ment adverbs.
